summary: Matched 11 previously stubbed item functions across Lugia, Old Kuri, White Bea, Ness bat, Ness PK Thunder ball, Matadogas, Hitodeman, Great Fox laser, and Kirby item code. The PR also refined item attribute/item-var structs and function prototypes needed to express those matches cleanly. decomp.dev reported +1336 matched bytes and all 11 listed functions moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.

key_files.reason: Expanded/refined item attribute and item-var layouts for Lugia, Hitodeman, and White Bea so matched code could access named offsets instead of padding.
Implemented itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys and it_802D23F4 using Lugia attrs, life timer setup, animation update, hitlag callbacks, and physics state transition.
Implemented it_802E4558 and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim, including item animation/audio-style setup, grIceMt_801FA6D8 call, and attribute-driven item vars.
Implemented it_802AB3F0 with careful NULL handling and Vec3 output fallback initialization.
Implemented it_802EB1EC as a state/setup routine resetting item flags/vars and chaining into common item helpers.
Added attribute fields and implemented it_802D4494 using HSD_Randi to compute item var x84 from attrs x14/x18.
Implemented it_802F2810 with accessory callback assignment and item transition/setup helper calls.
Implemented itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys with collision/ground-normal velocity handling.
Implemented it_2725_Logic58_PickedUp using itcmd vars to select animation update mode.
Implemented it_802CB798 using a common projectile/item spawn setup pattern shared with Lugia.
Updated it_802D4494 prototype from unknown to void(Item_GObj*).
Updated it_802D1830 prototype from unknown to void(Item_GObj*), enabling typed call from Lugia physics.

what_changed: Replaced 11 placeholder comments with matching C implementations: itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802D23F4, itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys, itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802E4558, it_2725_Logic58_PickedUp, it_802AB3F0, it_802CB798, it_802D4494, it_802EB1EC, and it_802F2810.
Expanded itLugiaAttributes from two floats to five floats at offsets +00, +04, +08, +0C, and +10; Lugia physics uses attrs->x10 and setup uses attrs->x0.
Refined itLugia_ItemVars by splitting padding to expose x60 at ip+E34, used as a countdown in itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys before zeroing z velocity and calling it_802D1830.
Refined itHitodeman_ItemVars to expose x84 at ip+E58 and updated local Hitodeman attributes to include x14 and x18; it_802D4494 stores a randomized value into x84.
Expanded itWhiteBeaAttributes with padding and x14, used by itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim to initialize whitebea item vars.
Added includes where matched functions needed typed declarations: it_2725.h in itgreatfoxlaser.c and it_26B1.h/it_2725.h/item.h in itkirby_2F23.c.
Updated headers for more specific prototypes: it_802D4494(Item_GObj*) and it_802D1830(Item_GObj*).
decomp.dev bot confirmed 11 new matches, +1336 matched bytes, and a small .sdata2 improvement in ithitodeman.
smart_moves: Used struct-layout improvements to turn raw offsets into meaningful fields just enough to match the functions, rather than leaving accesses as opaque padding.

Recognized repeated item setup idioms: setting xD44_lifeTimer from attrs, calling it_80274740, Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, assigning efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll, and calling it_8026B3A8 appears in both Lugia and Matadogas setup functions.
Preserved compiler-sensitive stack layout with PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(16) where needed for 100% matches.
For it_802AB3F0, matched the original defensive control flow by checking gobj, user_data, and out separately, including zero-vector fallback writes in z/y/x order.
For Ness bat pickup logic, collapsed four itcmd vars with bitwise OR to choose animation mode, matching the concise branch condition in the original.
For Hitodeman randomization, exposed attrs x14/x18 and used HSD_Randi((s32)(attrs->x14 - attrs->x18)) plus float conversion to reproduce the random range assignment.
decomp_lessons: Small item functions often become matchable after adjacent item var/attribute structs expose the exact offsets read or written by the function.
When a function pattern mirrors another item's lifecycle setup, reuse the same helper-call sequence and callback assignments as evidence for naming and typing.
Do not over-name unknown fields prematurely: this PR keeps offset-style names such as x60, x84, x10, and x14 while still improving layout enough to compile and match.
Typed prototypes in item headers can be necessary follow-through after matching a caller; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures helps reduce casts and makes future matches easier.
Item_GObj* versus HSD_GObj* should follow the observed function body and call sites; it_802F2810 accepts HSD_GObj* but casts to Item_GObj* for item helper calls.
Use GET_ITEM(gobj) or gobj->user_data consistently with local file patterns; both appear in matched code and may affect generated code shape.
For decomp progress records, bot reports are useful hard evidence of match count and byte gains, especially when there is no human review discussion.
naming_conventions: Matched functions with known motion roles keep descriptive names such as itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys, itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys, and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim.
Unknown item functions retain address-style names such as it_802D23F4, it_802E4558, it_802AB3F0, it_802CB798, it_802D4494, it_802EB1EC, and it_802F2810.
Struct fields with uncertain semantics remain offset-based names like x0, x10, x14, x18, x60, x84, xD44_lifeTimer, xDD4_itemVar, and xDCC_flag.
Padding fields are named by offset and adjusted as fields are discovered, e.g. pad_6C shrinking to expose x84, x0_pad shrinking to expose x60, and pad_x8 added before WhiteBeaAttributes x14.
Header comments preserve address labels, e.g. /* 2D4494 */ and /* 2D1830 */, while updating signatures.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) or PAD_STACK(16) in functions where stack shape mattered: it_802EB1EC, it_802D4494, it_802CB798, itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys, and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim.
Used explicit casts and pointer arithmetic in it_802AB3F0: ip = (Item*) ((u8*) ip + idx * 0x18), then copied pkthunder.xDEC to the Vec3 output.
Preserved write ordering for zeroing Vec3 fallback in it_802AB3F0 as z, y, then x, which may be compiler/order sensitive.
Used post-decrement countdown form in itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys: if (ip->xDD4_itemVar.lugia.x60-- != 0) to match original branch behavior.
Used bitwise OR over multiple itcmd vars in it_2725_Logic58_PickedUp rather than separate logical checks.
Matched common setup functions by exact call order, including Item_80268E5C modes, hitlag callback assignments, flag resets, and final transition/helper calls.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 56.03% (+0.03%, +1336 bytes), with 11 new 100% item-function matches.
Bot also reported one .sdata2 improvement in main/melee/it/items/ithitodeman from 37.50% to 57.89%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Lugia, White Bea, Hitodeman, or Great Fox laser xDD4_itemVar fields exposed here.
Search for other item setup functions that use the same lifeTimer + it_80274740 + Item_80268E5C + hitlag callback + it_8026B3A8 sequence.
Look for subsequent typing of grIceMt_801FA6D8 and whether it_802E4558 should include a stage/header declaration instead of a local extern.
Search it_802AB3F0 call sites to infer the meaning of idx * 0x18 and pkthunder.xDEC output.
Compare it_802F2810 with nearby Kirby item accessory setup functions to improve names for on_accessory and transition helpers.
